Hollowed Koada`Dal Rib Cage  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: SECONDARY
AC: 15
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
CHA: -15 WIS: +5 AGI: -5
SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5
WT: 6.0 Size: LARGE
Class: WAR CLR PAL SHD BRD ROG SHM
Race: ALL except HIE
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: SECONDARY
AC: 15
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.

SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5

Stackable:No

Tribute:96

Page Updated:Sat Sep 7th, 2019

Rarity: Common

[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Befallen
Item Lore: Hollowed Koada`Dal Ribcage


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
Looks
QuoteReply
# Apr 15 2003 at 12:31 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591788009928
83 posts
Looks like a ribcage when wielded. Would be nice if all items you look at can match the graphic when held.

Careful when drop tranferring this item. In Shadeweavers I couldn't pick it back up. Half of it was sticking out of the grass but I just could not target it to grab it. Stupid bug. I was more pissed off that I had to watch it poof in front of my useless attempts rather than losing the low ammount of cash I paid for it. Those Ulnaks do exactly the same thing. When I tried to drop transfer a rusty one (to my SK for his pet) in the same zone it just stuck out of the grass straight up with no way to pick it back up. **** I think I rather have someone swipe something on the spot rather than have something gay like this happen where you have to watch it disappear.



Edited, Tue Apr 15 13:00:54 2003
▲ Page top
Every now and again
QuoteReply
# May 08 2002 at 1:46 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591761222747
147 posts
Every now and again Verant will hold an EQ wide event. When this happens the residents of Befallen become mid 20s, and aggro on everything. If looking up the Bestiary for befallen about half the NPC's are not really there unless a GM event has been declared. The Dark elves, durring this event, all see thru invis.
▲ Page top
reverted again
QuoteReply
# Mar 07 2002 at 11:47 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253120327
27 posts
Befallen recently became the tougher version again,and a monk and I were cleaning out the third floor when this dropped.Since the monk all ready had one from a earlier drop,i got to loot it.I think its pretty cool and i will hold onto it,even i am a druid and they can't use it.
▲ Page top
Rectification
QuoteReply
# Oct 03 2001 at 4:48 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766233262
2 posts
Due to Verrant mismatch in source code when patching, the new Befallen Zone where the new object and mob where to be found has been erased and the old Befallen Zone re-installed instead.

So at the date of October the 3rd, the Beffalen Zone is the Old One and those objects are not dropping.

Nhindrazaar, Dwarven Cleric Follower of Brell Serillis in his 15th Circle

ummm ya this doesnt drop anymore or at least not that i know of i may be wrong but i doubt it my lvl 12 shammy has one and there badass.

Got one today 12-29-01 off of a reanimated sacrifice on the third floor. Mob looked like a female zombie.

# Sep 02 2001 at 1:43 AM Rating: Decent

GREETINGS! i have cleared the 3rd floor of befallen and have yet to see this reanimated sacrefice!i cannot find any info on the lvl or class or anything involving it.could anyone plz help with confirmed spawn points i have heard ther was 3 but i have yet to encounter this diabolical retch.i realy want this sheild!

terris-thule

▲ Page top
RE: help with spawn!
QuoteReply
# Sep 03 2001 at 11:56 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247593307
15 posts
Befallen seems to switch between the old 'newbie' dungeon, and the new 'tougher' dungeon. I'm not sure what triggers it. The new dungeon is the one with the reanimated sacrifices and the shield. Although, considering the ones I've gotten actually take away AC, I think its a pretty crappy shield.
▲ Page top
Seems bugged....
QuoteReply
# Aug 28 2001 at 1:25 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247593307
15 posts
This shield seems bugged. At least the ones dropped lately (as of 8/26/2001). When within my weight limit, this shield actually took away 10 AC over wearing no shield at all. I can't believe this is soley because of the -5 agi. Only when I was overburdened did this shield help, giving me about 15 AC (can't remember exactly) over nothing at all, and about 2 AC over a charred guardian shield. Very strange.
▲ Page top
RE: Seems bugged....
QuoteReply
# Aug 31 2004 at 5:05 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592343673941
161 posts
Agility makes a big difference. Example. Cougarskin Mask.

https://everquest.allakhazam.com/db/item.html?item=4236

I have that on my rogue, its obviously more AC then my Mask of Deception ....

https://everquest.allakhazam.com/db/item.html?item=1875

How much more AC right? Believe it or not It gives me almost 10AC. I think it goes on Defense and Agility. So when encumbered you lose Agility, AC drops, fighting when encumbered is not the best idea. A monk even gets another bonus he loses, it drops insane amounts. My friend told me when Defense is high enough Agility ads something like 1.5 points per agility. dont quote me on that, but I know for a fact it gave me WELL over 6AC.

Since that talk I have been re-thinking my armor (Especially on my rogue) This item :

https://everquest.allakhazam.com/db/item.html?item=19241

Gives me WAY more then 4AC... a TON more. I think almost 12 or more.

Edited, Tue Aug 31 18:10:19 2004
▲ Page top
Still dropping
QuoteReply
# Aug 20 2001 at 7:35 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750106032

As of this posting it still drops. Picked one up last nite. Lemme just say thanks to Radditz of the ECI server for bowing repeatedly to Need before Greed and allowing others to get stuff to make their characters better to his own exclusion.

hmmm
QuoteReply
# Aug 04 2001 at 3:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591764951641
3 posts
this looks like a ribcage no? i hope so, because if it does im gonna pick one up for my 50 shaman, this really isnt a bad hybrid shield at all, i had a walrus bone shield that i sold for MOUNDS of money and it was ac 15 and +5 wis, it looked like the newer shield graphics, sort of like the south african war shields, heh maybe i can farm this thing for ectra cash as this skeleton ribcage shield look is hard to get.
▲ Page top
Clue
QuoteReply
# Aug 02 2001 at 1:36 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591763939769
168 posts
I've already run through 4 of these nifty shields on the 3rd level of Befallen.

The key is to simply farm the spawn points of the Reanimated Sacrifices (there are three of these points that I know of).

If Bloodshadow or Bobbinsnort(sp?) are at one of these 3 spawn points, grease 'em! The R. Sacrifices won't spawn there if onna dese useless named are hoggin' the spot :)

Besides being a nice shield, they have an awesome skeletal graphic. Gonna be sporting one of these on my cleric and my baby Iksar SK (I'll even put the Lumberjack Helm on him to complete the whole "Skeletor in black chain" look! Mwahahahaha!).

Khemosaabe "SPAM" Longbow (of Cazic Thule server)
Lvl 51 Ranger and Slayer-of-Bunny-Wabbits
"We'll walk ten paces, turn, then fire...got it?"
"...Duh...someone's been sleeping in MY bed!"
▲ Page top
There is good out there.
QuoteReply
# Aug 02 2001 at 12:16 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760858775
View User Forum
32 posts
I was in Befallen last night helping my buddy with the Monk quest and one of the people we were grouped had a guildy drop this on me. Quite possibly the best gift I could have ever received. She left as soon as she gave it to me so I never got to thank her. That is why I play. I always try to help a lower leveler with items when I can.

Thanks to that unknown warrior.

Drops off Reanimated Sacrifices on the 3rd floor, you see them the shield when they have one.

▲ Page top
not a bad shield at all..
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2001 at 3:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248587043
1 post
Okies..look at this shield...its not bad at all, even for older players...it may not be an everday sheild..but it has its uses...This thing has 15ac..thats 5 more than a Charred Guardian Shield..and this thing has +5 to sv fire...not too bad for only losing 5 wisdom..I was lucky enough to get one of these and I am keeping it..I can already see it will be useful at my next naggy raid..

Lilirith
▲ Page top
RE: not a bad shield at all..
QuoteReply
# Aug 10 2001 at 4:58 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591761913405
67 posts
You don't LOSE five wisdom, you GAIN five wisdom with this shield equipped. I got one of these and gave it to an Iksar Shammie guildmate, and he absolutely loves it...I would use it myself if I could (better than Crested MM and *way* better than Shiny Brass, obviously). Unfortunately pallies can't use a High Elf's ribcage as a shield...hehe I can't understand why that would be...

To those wondering if it has a ribcage graphic when equipped, yes of course it does.

Grimveld Grimforge
30th Level Paladin of Ayonae Ro
